item.title: Guess the choreography only with the sound
item.description: Edit: From what I can find out the source is a social media post from this organization/event https://originschampionship.com/ https://www.instagram.com/originschampionship/ Some of the discussion here also inspired me to share the suggestion to watch "Rumble" which is an awesome documentary on the broader topic of minority origins of Rock and Roll. It's free every few months but worth paying for. https://www.pbs.org/independentlens/documentaries/rumble/ https://m.imdb.com/title/tt6333080/

item.description: Concepcion Usuga - Colombia, lifts a snatch of 106kg (234lbs). The bob of the hair, the scream, the knee high jump is all so joyous. Get strong, get big, get loud, get joyous

item.dc:creator: Mirrormancer

item.title: REPORT: The Department of Health and Human Services claims they will NOT be making an autism registry after massive public outrage.
item.description: The Department of Health and Human Services denied that it would create an autism health registry after advocates for the autistic community expressed fear about reported plans for one. An HHS official insisted to The Independent that no such plans existed. A “real-world data platform will link existing datasets to support research into causes of autism and insights into improved treatment strategies,” the official said. Full source: https://www.the-independent.com/news/world/americas/us-politics/hhs-walks-back-talk-of-an-autism-database-b2739800.html Keep the pressure on and squeeze em like they're the last drop of toothpaste in the tube. If there's one pattern I'm noticing with the Orange regime, it's that they have been fumbling and also showing weakness, panic, instability and fear as well. Hegseth sounds like he's going to have a public meltdown, Rubio looks like he will have a panic attack at any moment, many courts and judges including conservative ones are ruling against Krasnov. He's slipping in the polls and throwing public fits online and there has been more infighting, all while he looks weak against China and Russia. Add on top of that the very real possibility that his mind is slipping and the news media is starting to cover it more and more. Remember, if his popularity in the polls tanks hard enough, some of those spineless jellyfish in your congress might start actually saying things more publicly. A few have already here and there. That Murkowski lady said they're scared of retaliation from him. So keep at it! Keep protesting, keep marching, keep sharing information, keep calling your reps, keep applying pressure on the media and most importantly, keep supporting any businesses and universities and public figures that are publicly standing up to the bullies. This can be won.
item.dc:creator: Mirrormancer

item.title: In this situation, the dog was in high hopes of a rescue
item.dc:creator: AdventurousDonut480

item.title: Orangutans are truly incredible creatures.
item.description:
Adult males can grow up to five feet tall (about 153 cm), but what’s really impressive is their arm span—it can stretch out to a whopping eight feet (244 cm)! These great apes can weigh as much as 300 pounds (136 kg), and they’re incredibly strong—capable of lifting around 500 pounds (227 kg) with ease.
Their bite packs a punch too, with a force of around 600 PSI, which is nearly double that of a leopard. Despite these jaw-dropping stats, orangutans are remarkably gentle.
As these photos show, they’re calm, peaceful beings who rarely show aggression and don’t pose a danger to humans. In fact, they’re more curious than confrontational—quiet giants of the forest.
item.dc:creator: LunaAtlas

item.title: What Happened Today #86
item.description: ?✝️✈️ Trump met Zelenskyy knee-to-knee inside St. Peter’s Basilica, a rare moment of solemn diplomacy, in a sacred space. At home, the brutality never paused: ICE raids tore through cities. Citizen children were deported, including a four-year-old with stage four cancer and no medical assistance. A Coast Guardsman’s wife was arrested over an old immigration charge while settling into base housing. DOJ privacy rules were shredded to humiliate a sitting judge. The American people see the truth. They are rejecting Trump’s chaos, and the latest bellwether NY Times-Siena College poll shows they are not alone. Don’t trust the algorithm. Get this in your inbox every night, free and private, so you never miss what’s really going on. Add your email at https://govbrief.today #GovBriefToday #Resist Links for What Happened Today #86 1. https://go.govbrief.today/trump-zelenskyy-vatican 2. https://go.govbrief.today/citizens-exiled 3. https://go.govbrief.today/coastguard-wife 4. https://go.govbrief.today/west-texas-deportations 5. https://go.govbrief.today/weather-translations 6. https://go.govbrief.today/iran-talks 7. https://go.govbrief.today/operation-tidal-wave 8. https://go.govbrief.today/free-canal-fees 9. https://go.govbrief.today/patel-dugan-image 10. https://go.govbrief.today/nyt-siena-100days

item.title: Virginia Giuffre, one of Epstein and Prince Andrew's accusers, recently died "by suicide."
item.description: No specific means of her suicide has yet been given. Her family officially gave her death as suicide, but they may have been threatened to do so. On March 31, 2025, she put on IG that she was driving and collided with a bus at 70 mph and went into kidney failure. The hospital gave her four days to live. It all sounds incredibly suspicious to me. She was in a custody battle for her children at the time of her death, which doesn't sound suicidal to me. Sounds like she had set herself to a purpose. RIP, Giuffre.
item.dc:creator: Lassannn

item.title: Lilyanthe Japanese cotton lawn
item.description:
Good morning! Today is my birthday ☺️ I am not saying how old I am so don’t ask. I have decided to celebrate with this stunning Japanese cotton lawn called Lilyanthe in delicate shades of periwinkle and off white
This design looks like a nod to the iconic William Morris print called Ianthe with its graceful, interlacing art nouveau style lines but instead of Ianthe this print depicts a flourish of graceful lilies.
The fabric has a slightly more generous hand than a Liberty lawn, it was soft, pleated well, drapes nicely and is easy to work with. I took care to line up the color bands across the center front and I positioned the darkest color right at the belly for a sense of gravity.
Quarter for scale because banana was too big
item.dc:creator: thereoncewasamanfromnantucket

item.title: Korean scientists have developed a groundbreaking technology that can transform colon cancer cells back into normal cells without killing them. This "undo button" approach, led by Professor Kwang-Hyun Cho
item.description: Korean scientists have developed a groundbreaking technology that can transform colon cancer cells back into normal cells without killing them. This "undo button" approach, led by Professor Kwang-Hyun Cho, utilizes a digital twin model of cell differentiation to identify molecular switches that can reverse the cancerous state. This research suggests a new direction in cancer treatment, potentially minimizing side effects and recurrence.
item.dc:creator: Joyika

item.title: Saving for my next annual review with my team
item.dc:creator: svardfiska

item.title: Shop Safety Pro tips
item.description:
A safe craftsman is a confident, experienced craftsman. Get your children familiar with the tools as early as possible. This will also give them much more time to heal and get used to their prosthetics while their brains are still young enough to re-wire themself before adulthood. [satire filter off] Never use a tool like this without robust eye protection, and this sort of cutting disc has an extremely high risk of injury, even among strong, and experienced adult woodworkers. Here's a video explaining the danger of this sort of product, specifically: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=IIQu1e8DGUw [satire filter on]
This is NOT safe! Neither person is wearing a condom. [satire filter off] Just like with a firearm, where they say "Know your target and beyond", you should always know where the blade will travel through to the end of your cut, to make sure you don't damage the surface underneath, your tool in case you hit someting it can't cut, yourself in case the tool catches something and kicks back, or a body part that happens to be in the path of blade travel, like a finger wrapped around an edge, or the spine of Carl "Sawhorse" Johnson, here. [satire filter on]
They say a woodworker can never have enough clamps. But God granted us with four limbs (aka "nature's clamps"), and sometimes you just gotta use what the good lord gave ya to get 'er done. [satire filter off] Unstable footing can be very dangerous. In this case, if the saw blade were to bind and buck the saw in an unexpected way, or if his weight shifted unexpectedly, his body isn't in a stable position to absorb or respond to this sudden movement. He could easily loose his balance and fall, holding on a spinning blade right near his wedding vegetables/family tackle. While modern saws have a mechanism that can brake the saw blade nearly as soon as it's turned off, not all saws do, so act as if any saw you're using doesn't. [satire filter on]
I see your "table saw" and raise you a "desk saw", complete with built-in storage. [satire filter off] At first glance, this may not seem any more dangerous than some vintage table saws, but it definitely does lack some design aspects that make even those old "real" table saws safer to use, not to mention many of the more modern safety features, like a riving knife or splitter, blade guard, or an acurate fence which not only helps get accurate cuts, but also helps reduce dangerous kickback. The location of the motor and belt/pulley is...not ideal for safety, even compared to most vintage table saws. [satire filter on]
Can't find the right tool? Use the wrong one! Imagine the time savings!! [satire filter off] This image shows someone using a drill bit, to drive a screw. While you can use a drill as a screwdriver, you would normally replace the spiral hole-making bit, called a "drill bit" , with a "driver bit" used to drive screws. These driver bits are designed to securely turn a screw, while a drill bit has sharp edges on its point, designed to remove material. This could not only damage the screw preventing you from driving it fully, or removing it easilly, but it could also chip off pieces of metal that could go flying into nearby unprotected eyes. [satire filter on]
[satire filter off again] I'm not clever enough to come up with some comedy for this one, but the issue with this one isn't actually what you might think it is. While it may look sketchy that the man's hand is inside the guard where the blade is, and that isn't really ideal, this scene is depicting showing the younger boy how to adjust the blade depth, to ensure both a clean, and safe cut. Ideally you'd only want the blade to protrude juuust a bit beyond the thickness of the material, so that there's less blade exposed where you can't easily see it. He's also holding the blade guard dlider back with his left hand to make this adjustment. I still wouldn't recommend this posture and hand position, because you should treat every power tool as if it could turn on, if it's unplugged or the battery is removed, this could just be a sort of necessary grip to hold the saw in a way to show the child how the adjustment controls work. That said... Hearing protection is also an important part of job safety. And hard yellow plastic electrical wire nuts do not provide sufficient noise reduction. ? [satire filter on]
Any chance you get to spray your crotch with high-velocity sawdust, take it! The ladies (and some guys...no shame) go wild for it! [satire filter off] This man is using a tool called a "miter saw". However, he's standing behind the tool instead of in front of it. This means that all of the sawdust will be blasted right towards him, instead of out the back of the saw. Also, he doesn't appear to be holding his workpiece safely. He's putting weight on a part of the workpiece that's extending off the side of the "table" of this saw, so the moment the cut is made, the workpiece could suddenly release, and it could make for a damaged end of the piece, or some other sudden, unexpected and unsafe movement. [satire filter on]
And don't forget guys, any time you can use a tool in a way that allows you to showcase your biceps, take the opportunity. [satire filter off] Again, I have no idea why this person is using the saw backwards. At least he's not sitting right in the path of sawdust, but still...There are controls, adjustments, and line-of-sight issues with using this type of saw from the back, so it's not adviseable. [satire filter on]
Ever wish you could have the smell of a barbeque follow you throughout the entire work day? Try this unique soldering iron grip! Burn your flesh for that hot-off-the-grill smell! [/satire filter off] She's holding the part of the soldering iron that gets hot. If you look at that aprt of the metal shaft, you can see it's discolored brown from being heated to extremely high temperatures that can/will severely burn your fingers. I don't mean "like a very bad sunburn", but more like "melting your flesh" kind of burn. Only hold on to the insulated handle. Learn about your tools before using them, including reading the manuals, to understand how to use them safely. [satire filter on]
No, wait... [satire filter off] OK, so this may not seem bad to someone brand new to woodworking and table saws. He's wearing eye protection, his hands are clear of the blade, the saw even has a riving knife to prevent pinching behind the blade. However...this sort of cut is called a cross cut. You can see in his left hand, he's using a "miter gauge", which is used to make accurate cross cuts. However, the "cutoff" side is a small piece of wood that will be trapped between the blade and the fence. A piece this short, front to back, is very likely to twist before it can clear the blade, and get pulled towards the blade. This can very easilly pull your hand into the blade, or throw that piece of wood at you fast enough to cause injury, or embed itself in a wall. Here is an informative video about this exact issue: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=F0jbg9LuDsc Here is a (blood free) example of one of the things that could go wrong here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=vN7G7G3gMeg [satire filter on]
When raw-dogging the table saw (no guards, no guides), make sure you have your offspring nearby so he can not only be traumatized by your screams, but also grab ahold if your soul as it leaves your body, to inherit your powers. [satire filter off] 1) Except for some very niche use cases, never wear gloves around tools with rotating blades or parts. It's the same reason you souldn't wear loose sleeves, baggy shirts, necklaces, or have low-hanging hair. 2) Passing wood over a table saw blade like this, without a guide in place to ensure it's passing through the cut straight, it a recipe for disaster. The blade is likely to bind if this long piece of wood is moved even slightly to the side. Ideally there would be a fence alongside the blade that this piece could run against to keep it straight. 3) Before making your cuts, visualize your entire cut path. Imagine where your hands will need to be as your wood passes over the blade, and where you will need to move them to not only remain a safe distance from the blade, but also continue to have a secure hold on your workpiece and cutoff. With this sort of cut, you'd want to have a "push block" so that you could continue providing forward and downward pressure, even as the last part of your workpiece enters the blade. The push block is meant to be sacrificial and allow the blade to cut into it, and not your hands, while still allowing you to hold the wood securely, and safely. Google "push block thin stock table saw" for some ideas for how to do this safety. There are push blocks you can make yourself, and some you could purchase. [satire filter on]
Welding helmets and goggles are for betas. Are you a beta? I didn't think so. Sunglasses are good enough for Alpha males. Keep looking rad, Alpha. [satire filter off] Sunglasses are not good enough, even for "Alpha males". Welding produces concentrated light so bright and powerful, that it can actually burn your eyes even when you are 30 feet away due to their incredibly powerful UV radiation output. Even just a single flash could cause permanent, major eye damage. [satire filter on]
Be like Buckethead, everyone's favorite avant garde guitarist, in all aspects of your daily life! [satire filter off] Plastic buckets are not impact rated. While it could seem like this could be safer than just safety glasses because it covers your whole face, the material is not design to stop pieces of metal or concrete from flying at your face at the speed of bullets. Also, the clouded plastic material of this bucket is surely negatively impacting his visibility. The preferred safety gear would be a full-face protective shield, (with safety glasses under it, wouldn't be a bad idea) and a respirator to protect from the airborn dust particles. [satire on]
Oh, you still believe in gravity, glober? Gravity doesn't exist as long as you don't believe it exists. [satire filter off] Remember what I said above about picturing your entire cut before you make it, so you can know where your tool and hands will be once you get to the end? The same holds true here: think about what will happen to your work pieces. Right now, this sheet of wood is fully supported by the two sawhorses. But once the cut is complete, they will be two separate pieces, and each piece will be balancing on just one sawhorse. And as you can see, they would be quite off-balance, not even including the weight of the saw and the worker's downward pressure. At some point, the support will be lost, and things will drop...possibly unexpectedly...and unexpected, sudden movements are always a risky danger! [satire filter on]
Want to meet God, kids? Give that big band saw, with way too much blade exposed, a nice huge. Make sure to lean in head first! [satire filter off] *sigh* The whole stock photo series this image is from was a whole shit show of "how not to stand when using a bandsaw that could cut you in half". He's got about 8" left of his cut to push forward, and about 8" of space before his forehead makes contact with the blade. [satire filter on]
Safety squints: engaged! [satire filter off] This tool is called a router, and it is one of the fastest-spinning tools in a typical woodworking shop. And with that high power and high speed, there's potential for a lot of things to go wrong. Sawdust, both fine and coarse, is thrown at very very high speeds. But additionally, sometimes things happen with the router's bit. Small chips could go flying, or sometimes a bit itself can come loose. Eye protection is a must. Also, routers are some of the loudest power tools in a woodworking shop. Of all the tools in a typical shop, a router is the one I'd say is the tippy-top when it comes to needing hearing protection. Not just because of it's volume, but because the things you use it for often require it to be running for quite a while. For example, this man is doing some carving in the wood, which is more than just a simple once-over cut. It's sustained use. And prolonged exposure to loud sound can be just as dangerous as a louder sound, in shorter bursts. [satire filter on]
Join the Kardashain/Jenner Woodworking Academy! Learn to cut wood with power tools just like Kendal Jenner cuts a cucumber! If she can do it, so can you! [satire filter off] In case you aren't familiar with the target of the joke, allow me to dissect the frog*. Here's Kendall Jenner cutting a cucumber. And it appears it is the first time she's ever done it. Or used a knife. Or seen anyone use a knife. Or seen a knife. https://www.youtube.com/shorts/npvs6dUboKo *"Humor can be dissected, as a frog can, but the thing dies in the process and the innards are discouraging to any but the purely scientific mind." - E.B. White
item.dc:creator: necrojoe

item.title: An Infographic of Recent Joanne Bullshit
item.description:
I keep seeing posts and comments about this woman here. People saying "oh I loved the books growing up can't I still love them? Can't we separate art from artist anymore?"
I'm nonbinary and no, in this instance we can't. Joanne isn't just a keyboard warrior making mean posts about minorities online. Her money FUNDED taking away trans rights across an entire country.
item.dc:creator: Yogsothott

item.title: Return of the Meme: 1343 (Star Trek Sunday VIII)
item.description:
Let's get this bread.
Woke up with anxiety at an 8 today, I think because of the dream I had. It wasn't a nightmare, but it was certainly...stressful kinda?
I dreamed I met some new friends: a married couple and their best friend who were on their way to Telluride and invited me along and, well, since I have no job I said fuck it, sure. The husband complained all the time about everything, just, wow, an absolute ball of joy. The wife had an affair with me and was glad to bang someone who enjoyed her, then she decided she wanted: nay, was required by the Fates, to bear my child. Her husband was mad that it wasn't HIS child that was required; he didn't care about the affair at all. A huge blow-out fight between everyone occurred, and I was left stranded while trying to find a hotel in an unfamiliar mountain town in Colorado, debating if the sex was good enough to end up bringing some sort of prophecy-child into existence while hiding from the husband who wanted to sacrifice me to The Old Gods. Anyway, welcome to my subconscious, I guess?
Hopefully at some point. It's okay if it never happens, though.
Pippin tax! Today's message from Pippin: "What the hell? What happened?" [Kiss noises] Pippin it's okay good boy." ______ 1) Are you on the run to a safe place and passing through Arizona on your way to safer grounds? My apartment is open to you to crash at, and it is a safe space. Pippin and I will make you a meal and gladly share tea, wine, or mead with you. 2) Hey! If you're a woman or minority in a STEM field in Arizona living north of Phoenix and you need new headshots for this website or another detailing your experiences/research/accomplishment because our monstrous regime has deleted your experience from an official government website, PM me. Pippin and I have a good Nikon DSLR, and we'll do the photos for free, and, uh, you'll get to meet Pippin, I guess? (If you know someone who could use this who's not on Imgur, feel free to PM me to get my email address for them and tell them they're free to message me.) 3) Take your meds with a full glass of water, please. Do it for Pippin, or Space Mom, or better yet, do it for yourself 4) Ĝefeloað wē. We will carry on.
item.dc:creator: CodyBurkett
